member.name: Graeme Downie
member.synopsis: Graeme Downie is the Labour MP for Dunfermline and Dollar , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Russia: Subversion
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, is he will define the meaning of "sub threshold" in relation to Russian threats and offensive action against UK people and interests.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Recent publications, including the House of Common’s Defence Committee ‘Defence in the Grey Zone’ report and the Strategic Defence Review, highlight the breadth and depth of threats posed by sub-threshold activity. Russia remains the most pressing and imm...

writtenQuestions.heading: Russia: Subversion
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what activities would not be considered "sub threshold" in relation to Russian threats and offensive action against UK people, businesses and interests.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We cannot provide detail on how the MOD would respond to certain activities versus others as this would assist our adversaries.

writtenQuestions.heading: Warships: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what assessment his Department has made of whether the design of the Common Combat Vehicles outlined in the Defence Investment Plan will be based on the Type-31 frigate.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Common Combat Vessel (CCV) will be the Royal Navy’s first ‘Hybrid’ warship, coordinating uncrewed systems in the air, on the surface and under the sea to deliver more resilient air defence at the centre of the Hybrid Navy. Whilst the CCV remains in the in...

writtenQuestions.heading: Military Aircraft: Deployment

writtenQuestions.answerText: The number of Quick Reaction Alert launches from either RAF Coningsby or RAF Lossiemouth in each month in 2025 and 2026 can be found below. Number of days QRA launched each month. 2025Number of days QRA launched each month. 2026Jan11Feb00Mar00Apr21May01J...

writtenQuestions.heading: Natural Gas: Energy Supply
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, when he expects to publish the outcome of the consultation entitled Gas system in transition: security of supply, published on 26 November 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department welcomes engagement on such an important issue and is making an assessment of all the evidence submitted, a response will be published in due course.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Tourism: Dunfermline

writtenQuestions.heading: Motor Sports: Scotland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of compliance with the Terrorism (Protection of Premises) Act 2025 on the Scottish Rally Championship.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Terrorism (Protection of Premises) Act 2025 establishes a tiered approach to requirements, with those responsible for premises and events in scope required to fulfil different requirements according to the number of individuals it is reasonable to exp...

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Power: Torness
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment his Department has made of whether Torness could host (a) Small Modular Reactors and (b) large-scale nuclear development.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In Autumn 2025, Great British Energy – Nuclear (GBE-N) was commissioned by Department Ministers to assess Scotland’s potential for new nuclear power, whilst the government has also tasked GBE-N with identifying suitable sites that could host a potential l...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Power: Scotland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, whether the National Energy System Operator has considered the potential for new nuclear development in Scotland as part of its Strategic Spatial Energy Plan.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Energy System Operator has been commissioned by the UK, Scottish and Welsh governments to produce the Strategic Spatial Energy Plan (SSEP) on a three-yearly basis. As per Scottish Government policy, the first SSEP will not be able to consider...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.answerText: We know that our national heritage remains a core attraction for international and domestic visitors, and I am pleased Dunfermline is exploring how its heritage assets and visitor attractions can work together to boost footfall as well as benefit the loca...

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence continues to engage regularly with Maritime Trade Unions on matters relating to pay and conditions for Royal Fleet Auxiliary personnel. It would not be appropriate to provide further detail on specific meetings while those discussi...

writtenQuestions.heading: Aviation: Compensation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what plans she has to update UK261 to protect UK air passengers travelling within the EU.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is clear that passengers should benefit from a high level of protection when travelling by air. UK261 provides equivalent passenger rights and protections to EU261, including rights to compensation and assistance in cases of delay, cancella...

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Media: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment her Department has made of the extent to which social media platforms’ moderation standards reflect UK standards of age-appropriateness.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Under the Online Safety Act, social media platforms are required to protect their users from illegal content, and children from content that is harmful. This includes taking measures, such as content moderation, to protect children from ‘priority’ content...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Media: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment her Department has made of the use of artificial intelligence by social media platforms to support effective content moderation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Under the Online Safety Act, platforms have duties to protect their users from illegal content and children from content that is harmful.Where proportionate, services will be required to take steps such in areas including content moderation. It is for Ofc...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology
